stem: Which of the following is equivalent to $(x + 3)(x - 5)$?
explanation:
Use FOIL to expand $(x + 3)(x - 5)$:

First: $x \cdot x = x^2$
Outer: $x \cdot (-5) = -5x$
Inner: $3 \cdot x = 3x$
Last: $3 \cdot (-5) = -15$

Combining all terms: $x^2 - 5x + 3x - 15$

Combining like terms ($-5x + 3x = -2x$): $x^2 - 2x - 15$

The equivalent expression is $x^2 - 2x - 15$.

distractorRationale.A: Sign error on the middle term: the student added $-5x + 3x$ as if both were positive, getting $+2x$ instead of $-2x$.
distractorRationale.C: Sign error on the constant: the student computed $3 \times (-5) = +15$ instead of $-15$.
distractorRationale.D: Incomplete FOIL: the student only multiplied the First and Last terms ($x \cdot x$ and $3 \cdot (-5)$), omitting the Outer and Inner cross-terms entirely.
